samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Chip clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 16 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 16 bits. Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits. Chip output flag signal indicating the completion of the multiplication operation. Shift count register to track the number of shifts performed during multiplication. Reset behavior

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the shift count register (i) is set to 0.

Count behavior

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated.

When the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, the shift count register (i) increments by 1.

Reset behavior

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) are reset to 0.

Initialization

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) are stored in the respective registers (areg and breg) when the shift count register (i) is 0.

Shift and accumulate operation

For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, the product register (yout_r) accumulates the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.

Output assignment

The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r).
